title: Credit and its sources
description: Practice understanding of credit and different sources for borrowing

assessment_items.question_content:
Lucia took out a $\$500$ loan to help with her medical bills. By the time she made her final payment, she had paid back a total of $\$540$.

**What is the additional $\$40$ she paid known as?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Jamie borrowed $\$800$ to fix their car. After paying off the loan, they realized they had paid a total of $\$880$.

**What is the extra $\$80$ they paid called?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Lila borrowed $\$1{,}200$ to buy a car. After completing all her payments, she realized she had paid a total of $\$1{,}350$.

**What is the name for the extra $\$150$ she paid?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_  is a short and expensive loan that is backed by the title of a vehicle, like a car, a truck, or a motorcycle.

[[☃ radio 3]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_  is a flexible and convenient loan that is not backed by anything.

[[☃ radio 3]]

**Choose the best term to fill in the blank.**  

>A \_\_\_\_\_\_  is a short and expensive loan that is meant to give cash until the next payday and requires you to give the lender a post-dated check.

[[☃ radio 3]]

The specifics of a loan, such as interest rate, payment structure, and duration, can differ significantly from one to another.

**Which of these loans is likely to have the *lowest* interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The specifics of a loan, such as interest rate, payment structure, and duration, can differ significantly from one to another.

**Which of these loans is likely to have the *highest* interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The specifics of a loan, such as interest rate, payment structure, and duration, can differ significantly from one to another.

**Which of these loans is likely to have the *highest* interest rate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You need to fix your roof, because it is leaking water and causing damage to your house. You have a fair credit score and a car you rarely drive. The roof repair costs $\$1{,}500$, and you can pay it off in $12$ months. 

**Which loan would be the best choice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

A power surge in the area fried your electronics. You need to replace your fridge and TV. You have a good credit score and a job. The total cost is $\$1{,}700$, and you can pay it off in $9$ months. 

**Which loan would be the best choice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

At a recent dentist appointment, you were told you need a root canal done. You have a fair credit score, and a car you rarely drive. The total cost for a root canal is $\$700$, and you can pay it off in $6$ months. 

**Which loan would be the best choice?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
